publishedAbstract: A memory corruption flaw, which may allow unauthorized remote code execution, was found in the method Network Security Services (NSS) verified certificates. The issue is assigned [CVE-2021-43527](https://access.redhat.com/security/cve/cve-2021-43527) and has been rated with a severity impact of Critical.
publishedTitle: NSS Memory corruption when decoding DSA signatures - CVE-2021-43527
